title: PBMC Isolation Protocol from Whole Blood
author: Adil R. Sarhan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bnhxmb7n

steps.instruction: Collect 3 x 6 mL, blood into BD Vacutainer sodium heparin 6ml tubes, green BD Hemogard Closure 13x100mm (BD, cat# 367876). Mix gently by inverting tubes 8 - 10 times.

Add density gradient medium Ficoll-Paque PREMIUM to the SepMate™ tube (STEMCELL, Cat# 85450, 50 mL capacity) using a 20ml syringe with a large bore needle (at least 21G) by carefully adding the 15 mL through the central hole of the SepMate™ insert. Dispose of needle appropriately. The top of the density gradient medium will be above the insert. NOTE: Small bubbles may be present in the density gradient medium after pipetting. These bubbles will not affect performance.
Using a 5 ml pipette, transfer 15 mL, blood from the BD Sodium heparin tubes into a 50 ml Falcon tube and dilute at a 1:1 ratio by adding 15 mL, PBS with 2% FBS . Mix gently by inversion.

Keeping the SepMate™ tube vertical, add the diluted blood sample by pipetting it down the side of the tube. Take care not to pour the diluted blood sample directly through the central hole that lies in the centre of the insert disk.
Centrifuge at 1200 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes , with the brake ON.
After centrifugation, carefully pipette off the top plasma transparent layer and discard appropriately.
Using a new pipette, collect the remainder of the sample above the insert disk (which contains the PBMCs) into a 15 mL falcon tube.
Top up the tube to 15 mL, PBS with 2% FBS . Mix gently by inversion.
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes , brake ON, to pellet the PBMCs.
Carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the PBMC pellet.
Carefully resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL, PBS with 2% FBS .
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes , brake ON to pellet the PBMCs.
During the centrifugation Step 12, take MLi-2 inhibitor stock solution (200 µM / 1000x) out of the -80 °C freezer, and leave at Room temperature to thaw for subsequent use (Steps 19 and 21)
Immediately after the centrifugation, carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the PBMC pellet.
Resuspend the PBMC cell pellet in 10 mL, PBS with 2% FBS by gently pipetting cells up and down 4 times.
Divide resuspended PBMCs equally into 2 tubes ( 5 mL in each).
Label one tube “DMSO” and other tube “MLi-2”.
To “DMSO” labelled tube, add 5 µL, DMSO and mix gently by pipetting up and down 2 times with a 10 ml pipette.
To “MLi-2” labelled tube, add 5 µL, of 200 µM MLi-2 stock solution (final concentration 200 nM) and mix gently by pipetting up and down 2 times with a 10 ml pipette.
During the incubation period:
Incubate samples for 00:30:00 at Room temperature . Mix gently by inversion every 00:10:00 during the incubation period.
Remove 0.5 M DIFP stock from -80 °C freezer and place in fume hood On ice .
Remove 1 mg/ml Microcystin-LR stock from -80 °C freezer and place at Room temperature to thaw.
Defrost an aliquot ( 0.5 mL ) of the lysis buffer by taking it out of the freezer allow to defrost at Room temperature and then place it On ice for subsequent use (Step 27)
Prepare 1 mL, PBS with 2% FBS containing 1 µL, DMSO and call this “DMSO Resuspension Buffer”
Prepare 1 mL, PBS with 2% FBS containing 1 µL, 200 µM MLi-2 stock solution and call this “MLi-2 Resuspension Buffer”

After the 00:30:00 incubation, centrifuge both tubes at 335 x g, °C, 5 minutes . The setting for acceleration and deceleration is both 5 using a Beckman Coulter Allegra X-15R Centrifuge (maximum is 10) to pellet the PBMCs.
Carefully discard the supernatant in each tube without disturbing the PBMC pellets.
For the DMSO labelled sample gently resuspend pellet in 1 mL, “DMSO resuspension buffer” and transfer to Eppendorf tube labelled “DMSO”.
For the MLi-2 labelled sample gently resuspend pellet in 1 mL, “MLi-2 resuspension buffer” and transfer to Eppendorf tube labelled “MLi-2”.
Centrifuge both tubes at 300 x g, °C, 3 minutes .

During this time proceed with preparing the lysis buffer: In the fume hood carefully to the 0.5 mL, lysis buffer add 0.5 µL, 0.5 M DIFP solution as well as 0.5 µL, 1 mg/ml microcystin-LR . Mix and leave On ice until use. DIFP should be added to lysis buffer within 00:15:00 of cell lysis (Steps 31 and 32) as DIFP relatively unstable in aqueous solution.

steps.instruction: Return 0.5M DIFP to -80 °C freezer.
Immediately after the centrifugation in Step 26, carefully discard supernatant by using a pipette while taking care not to disturb the PBMC pellets.
Place the tubes with the PBMC cell pellets On ice .

Immediately add 200 µL, lysis buffer containing DIFP to the “DMSO” labelled tube. Using a 100-200 µL pipette, re-suspend the cell pellet by pipetting up and down until all cells are lysed (5-10 times).
Next add 200 µL, lysis buffer containing DIFP to the “DMSO” labelled tube. Using a 100-200 µL pipette, re-suspend the cell pellet by pipetting up and down until all cells are lysed (5-10 times).
Incubate cell lysates On ice for 00:10:00 . Place “DMSO” and “MLi-2” labelled tubes into a centrifuge and remove cell debris by centrifugation at 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes .
Transfer ”DMSO” and “MLi-2” supernatants (containing PBMC lysates) into new Eppendorf tubes. Discard debris pellet.
Immediately snap freeze samples in liquid nitrogen.
Store samples at -80 °C until further use or shipment on dry ice keeping samples frozen at all times.
